evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Championing reliability and efficiency through comprehensive monitoring
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (reliability, fault-tolerance, observability, governance, least privilege) saturate the JD with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Deep understanding of privacy, governance, and least privilege principles
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Security threat detection with privacy, governance, and least-privilege principles directly implicates compliance and risk exposure at scale, placing this at the highest risk tier.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Building AI-ready data infrastructure on the semantic layer using Snowflake
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Petabyte-scale data handling and an established semantic layer at a large, well-known company indicate a mature, specialised data organisation.
evidence.jd_authorship: Knowledge how to build fault-tolerant, real-time and batch pipelines using dbt, Kafka, and streaming frameworks
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise architectural and scale terms (Snowflake, Kafka, streaming frameworks, petabyte-scale) give a concrete picture of the daily engineering work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Enabling security teams to make informed decisions with data
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centres on serving the security team as internal data consumers via self-service infrastructure, an internal_data pattern.
evidence.autonomy_level: Building leverage, including with AI, for self-service capabilities
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Building leverage and AI-ready infrastructure is strategic-leaning, but the role is also explicitly framed as enabling and supporting security teams' decisions, giving mixed autonomy.
evidence.ai_role: Building AI-ready data infrastructure on the semantic layer using Snowflake
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds AI-ready data infrastructure that downstream AI systems consume, making AI the consumer of the candidate's engineering output.
evidence.testing_framing: Care deeply about data reliability, cost efficiency, and observability
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Reliability and observability are framed as an owned, championed responsibility ("championing reliability... through comprehensive monitoring"), not just a tool in a skills list.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Powering real-time threat detection by building resilient, high-throughput pipelines
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Security threat detection, privacy, governance, and least-privilege framing dominate the JD, reflecting fear of security/compliance failures rather than simple pipeline outages.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Powering real-time threat detection by building resilient, high-throughput pipelines
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes building new AI-ready infrastructure and self-service capabilities with engineering at scale on existing petabyte-scale security data systems.
evidence.loss_aversion: Deep understanding of privacy, governance, and least privilege principles — reflects fear of security/privacy failures and unauthorized access rather than simple pipeline outages.
